channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. and Iran Exchange Fresh Strikes
item.description: The United States launched a new wave of airstrikes on Iran early Thursday, and Iran claimed to have retaliated, bringing the two sides close to a return to all-out war.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Enghelab Square in central Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Inside the Knicks’ Historic Comeback to Take Game 4 of the N.B.A. Finals
item.description: Hear instant reaction to the New York Knicks’ win over the San Antonio Spurs.
item.dc:creator: Dave DuFour, Mike Vorkunov and Andrew Schlecht

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Amazon.com of South Korea’ Is Fined a Record $409 Million
item.description: South​ Korea​’s regulatory investigation of Coupang, an e-commerce giant incorporated in the United States, has led to diplomatic tension between Seoul and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:credit: Anthony Wallace/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Sorting through Coupang packages in Seoul last year. The company has become part of the urban fabric of South Korea.

item.title: World Cup Kicks Off in Mexico
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup begins on Thursday with a match between Mexico and South Africa. The tournament takes place in three countries: the United States, Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Luis Cortes/Reuters

item.title: Knicks Fans Rejoice After Game 4 Victory
item.description: Fans and celebrities, including Taylor Swift and Timothée Chalamet, celebrated after the Knicks’ record comeback to win Game 4 of the N.B.A finals
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times

item.title: La nueva táctica con drones de Ucrania está dañando a Rusia
item.description: Los ataques de alcance medio, con drones mejorados que Ucrania produce en grandes cantidades, están provocando escasez de combustible y complica la rotación de soldados.
item.dc:creator: Marc Santora

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Soldados preparándose para lanzar drones contra objetivos en Rusia desde una ubicación no revelada en Ucrania el mes pasado.

item.title: A Fractured North America Begins a Historic World Cup
item.description: Mexico and Canada have faced significant recent tension in their relations with the United States. But in their historic World Cup bid, the three countries promoted teamwork.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mexico City’s iconic stadium will host the opener for a record third time, after undergoing a near $200 million makeover for this World Cup and increasing its capacity to 87,500 seats.

item.title: With Inflation Bearing Down, Europe Is Facing Higher Interest Rates
item.description: The European Central Bank is set to raise rates, the first such move since September 2023, as energy disruptions caused by the Iran war drive higher prices.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson

media:content.media:credit: Samuel Aranda for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The European Central Bank is expected to raise its forecast for inflation as the war pushes prices higher.

item.title: New York City Leaders Propose Building More Homes on Top of Libraries
item.description: Julie Menin, the City Council speaker, unveiled plans for three projects combining affordable housing developments and library renovations. It is part of a nationwide trend.
item.dc:creator: Mihir Zaveri
item.category: Department of Housing Preservation and Development (NYC)

media:content.media:credit: Heather Khalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Julie Menin, the City Council speaker, said combining housing development with library renovations was a cost-effective way to address two of New Yorkers’ priorities.

item.title: City Council Calls for Tighter Review of N.Y.C. School System Contracts
item.description: The chancellor of the city’s schools, Kamar Samuels, is being investigated over a $180,000 no-bid deal dating to his previous role.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kamar Samuels, the chancellor of New York City schools, has acknowledged a “lapse in procedure” in the awarding of a no-bid contract when he was serving a prior role.

item.title: The Mystery Artist Filling Subway Ad Space With Whimsy
item.description: Sue Sarah Gilbert, a Rockefeller descendant in Seattle, raised $1 million to place her drawings in New York City stations.
item.dc:creator: Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:credit: Michelle V. Agins/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sue Sarah Gilbert, a self-described hobby artist, raised money from investors and contributed some of her own to fund a monthslong campaign displaying her artwork in several subway stations.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 11, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Trade Fresh Attacks
item.description: The continued exchanges of fire have raised fears that the two sides could return to all-out war.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times

item.title: Desi Lydic Does a Spit Take at the Price of Trump’s Gold Coin
item.description: “$12,000? For a coin? Does it come with a used Honda Civic?” Lydic said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Word of the Day: dudgeon
item.description: This word has appeared in one article on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Nadie gana en la guerra en Medio Oriente
item.description: Ninguno de los que han participado en las guerras recientes en Medio Oriente puede declararse vencedor. Todos son culpables.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Fotoilustración de Tam Stockton para The New York Times

item.title: It’s Godzilla vs. T. Rex Out There All Over America
item.description: Is there nothing voters won’t forgive?
item.dc:creator: Frank Bruni and Bret Stephens

media:content.media:credit: Mark Peterson for The New York Times

item.title: The War in Ukraine Has Now Gone On Longer Than World War I
item.description: Parallels between the two wars abound, from the grinding nature of the fighting to the way new technologies reshaped warfare.
item.dc:creator: Constant Méheut

media:content.media:credit: Tyler Hicks/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Ukrainian soldier in Kostiantynivka, a city ravaged by drone warfare, in January.

item.title: Medicare Advantage Plans Often Deny Seniors Access to Special Care, Analysis Shows
item.description: Two reports by U.S. investigators reveal how Medicare Advantage is quick to reject requests for short-term nursing home or inpatient rehab services.
item.dc:creator: Reed Abelson
item.category: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services

media:content.media:credit: David Goldman/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Two reports found that the three major insurers that sell Medicare Advantage plans denied about 13 percent of patients’ requests to go to a skilled nursing facility to recover from surgery or a serious illness.

item.title: A.I. and the Chip Boom: Take a Quiz on New Korean Slang and Memes
item.description: What’s a “silicon-collar”? South Korea’s chip industry has been supercharged by the advent of the A.I. age, inspiring a host of new phrases.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim

media:content.media:credit: Anthony Wallace/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Quote of the Day: U.S. Immigration Policy Blocks Some From Soccer’s Global Stage
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, June 11, 2026.

item.title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I.
item.description: At The New York Times’s Hard Fork Live event, Mr. Nadella addressed the backlash against artificial intelligence and President Trump’s comments about Americans sharing in the wealth of A.I. companies
item.dc:creator: Emmy Martin

media:content.media:credit: Chona Kasinger for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Satya Nadella, the chief executive of Microsoft, at an event the Microsoft campus last year.

item.title: What’s Love Got to Do With the Movie 'Obsession’? | Cannonball with Wesley Morris
item.description: The hit movie has a troubling take on relationships.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Morris and Angelica Jade Bastién

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photo: Focus Features
media:content.media:description: Nikki and Bear in “Obsession”

item.title: What’s Love Got to Do With Obsession?
item.description: Wesley Morris and pop culture critic Angelica Jade Bastièn discuss the hit horror movie “Obsession”’and what it says about relationships today.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Rocklin, Eddie Costas, John White, Felice Leon and Austin Mitchell

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photo: Focus Features
media:content.media:description: Nikki and Bear in “Obsession”

item.title: They Know About Loss. They’re Rooting for a Knicks Win.
item.description: Inspired by the words of Karl-Anthony Towns, Sparrow Funeral Home in Brooklyn, hosted a watch party for basketball fans and grieving neighbors alike.
item.dc:creator: Maya King and Shuran Huang

media:content.media:description: More than 70 people turned out to Sparrow Funeral Home for a Knicks watch party for basketball fans who are also missing someone they hoped to have experienced this moment with.

item.title: EE. UU. pidió a Colombia que cancelara la reunión de Petro con Mamdani
item.description: El Departamento de Estado había cancelado la visa del presidente Gustavo Petro el año pasado después de que asistió a una manifestación a favor de Palestina en Manhattan. Tenía previsto asistir a un foro organizado por el alcalde de Nueva York, Zohran Mamdani.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal, Edward Wong and Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Angela Weiss/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: El presidente de Colombia, Gustavo Petro, durante una reunión del Consejo de Seguridad de las Naciones Unidas en Nueva York el miércoles.

item.title: How a High-Powered Gathering Turned Into a Knicks Watch Party
item.description: An event for some of New York’s elite shifted gears when a TV rolled into the bar.
item.dc:creator: Dionne Searcey and Adrienne Grunwald

media:content.media:description: Watching Game 4 of the N.B.A. finals beside a candlelit spread.

item.title: No, There Was Not a Snake Invasion at the Swiss Team’s World Cup Training Facility
item.description: A map of the San Diego facility that the team is using as its base led people to believe that snakes had infiltrated the premises.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:credit: Denis Poroy/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Switzerland’s training base for this three-country World Cup will be in San Diego.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 11, 2026
item.description: Nikhil Bailey’s debut puzzle is a numbers game.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Simon Simard for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Test your luck on this Thursday puzzle. No snake eyes! No snake eyes!

item.title: U.S. Officials Told Colombia to Cancel President’s Meeting With Mamdani
item.description: The State Department canceled President Gustavo Petro’s visa last year after he attended a pro-Palestinian rally in Manhattan. He had planned to attend a forum led by Mayor Mamdani of New York.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal, Edward Wong and Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Angela Weiss/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: President Gustavo Petro of Colombia during a United Nations Security Council meeting in New York on Wednesday.

item.title: Oil Prices Rise as U.S. Launches New Attack on Iran
item.description: Oil prices jumped after the United States and Iran exchanged fire for a second day, heightening fears that the two countries could slide back into open conflict.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Scott Olson/Getty Images

item.title: Lee Raymond, Who Created Global Oil Behemoth Exxon Mobil, Dies at 87
item.description: He oversaw Exxon’s acquisition of a rival, cut costs relentlessly and denied the scientific consensus on climate change.
item.dc:creator: James R. Hagerty

media:content.media:credit: Donna McWilliam/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Lee Raymond, the chairman and chief executive of Exxon Mobil Corp., at a news conference in 2005. A former high school debating champion, he was known for making withering remarks to those who challenged him.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Haim Sisters Appear at MSG for Knicks Game
item.description: The musician, who is believed to be planning a wedding in the city, showed up in a musical take on Knicks gear.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Taylor Swift and Este and Alana Haim came to support the Knicks.

item.title: Burning Cross Is Found in Downtown Chicago Park
item.description: No one was injured by the burning cross, which was found in Grant Park, the authorities said. The police and the F.B.I. are investigating.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald and Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: Google Maps
media:content.media:description: The intersection of Balbo and Columbus Drives in Chicago, near where the burning cross was found.

item.title: Bill Gates dice que Epstein intentó usar sus aventuras extramatrimoniales en su contra
item.description: El filántropo multimillonario testificó el miércoles en una audiencia a puerta cerrada del Congreso de EE. UU. sobre la investigación del Departamento de Justicia sobre Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Goldstein
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

media:content.media:credit: Pete Marovich para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Las revelaciones sobre la conexión de Bill Gates con Jeffrey Epstein salieron a la luz después de que Epstein fuera detenido en 2019.

item.title: Trump’s Plaques on the Presidential ‘Walk of Fame,’ Fact-Checked and Annotated by Historians
item.description: President Trump’s ‘Walk of Fame’ has an unreliable narrator. We asked eight historians what they thought.
item.dc:creator: Larry Buchanan, Karen Yourish, Doug Mills, Jon Huang and Raj Saha

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Analysis of Satellite Image and Videos Suggest Precision U.S. Strikes on Iranian Water Facility
item.description: It is unclear if the U.S. intentionally struck the facility or knew what it was. Deliberately targeting civilian infrastructure could constitute a war crime.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl and Christiaan Triebert
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

item.title: Top Pentagon Official Worked Closely With C.I.A. Officer Later Found With Gold Bars
item.description: David Rush, the C.I.A. employee, worked on a highly classified China spying program with Stephen A. Feinberg, the Pentagon’s second-ranking official.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Stephen A. Feinberg in February 2025, during his confirmation hearing to be deputy defense secretary.

item.title: Hot Cars and Stolen Crypto: A Canadian Teen Pocketed Millions, Prosecutors Say
item.description: A case illustrates both the low-hanging opportunities new financial technologies offer to scammers and the downside of speeding in a Rolls-Royce.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin

media:content.media:credit: Saul Martinez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The C. Clyde Atkins U.S. Courthouse in Miami. Trenton Johnston, a Canadian, pleaded guilty in federal court to one count of conspiracy to commit money laundering.

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times

item.title: Need Last-Minute Courtside Knicks Tickets? Having a Luxury Lifestyle Adviser Helps.
item.description: The wealthy have descended on New York for top basketball, golf and soccer events, and they need prime tickets.
item.dc:creator: Dionne Searcey

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: New York’s V.I.P. concierge services were buzzing this week with requests for not just any tickets to the Knicks game — they need to be courtside and come with club access.

item.title: Knicks Fans Greet Game 4 Win With Chaotic Energy
item.description: Taylor Swift and Timothée Chalamet were among the celebrities celebrating courtside in New York after a record comeback moved the Knicks within a win of a long-awaited N.B.A. title.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times

item.title: Unusually Warm Weather in Bay Area Prompts Heat Alert and Wildfire Concerns
item.description: High temperatures across the San Francisco Bay Area will reach into the 90s on Thursday, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: Lea Suzuki/San Francisco Chronicle, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Some parts of the Bay Area could reach close to 100 degrees on Thursday, the National Weather Service said.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama, the Knicks’ Biggest Foe and New York’s Biggest Tourist
item.description: Mr. Wembanyama has had to keep a low profile while in New York to try and win the N.B.A. finals, but on visits there, he has always resisted hiding in hotel rooms and private cars.
item.dc:creator: Tania Ganguli

media:content.media:credit: Al Bello/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: On visits to New York City, Victor Wembanyama has played chess in Washington Square Park, taken the subway and drawn in Gramercy Park.

item.title: Canada Moves to Ban Social Media Use for Youth Under 16
item.description: The country’s previous attempt to get tech companies to shelter young users failed amid heavy criticism from civil liberty groups.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:credit: Fred Thornhill/Reuters
media:content.media:description: People holding up cellphones at a concert in Toronto.

item.title: La OPI de SpaceX hará millonarios a 4400 de sus empleados
item.description: Elon Musk podría convertirse pronto en un billonario, pero la salida a bolsa de su empresa de cohetes también extenderá una riqueza inesperada a miles de sus empleados y exempleados.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grind

media:content.media:credit: Chelsea Beck
media:content.media:description: Ilustración de un grupo de personas montadas en un cohete que asciende impulsado por billetes mientras ellas van echando más billetes desde bolsas con el signo de dólares.

item.title: Pope Leo Blesses the Sagrada Familia
item.description: Pope Leo XIV inaugurated the Sagrada Familia basilica’s newly completed central tower on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:credit: Dan Kitwood/Getty Images

item.title: Storms Knock Out Power for Hundreds of Thousands in the Midwest
item.description: Forecasters warned that destructive storms were expected across the region into Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: FIFA President Tells World Cup Critics to ‘Chill’ About Concerns
item.description: With the World Cup facing growing crises, Gianni Infantino, the FIFA president, struck a defiant tone on the eve of the competition’s start.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Carl Recine/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: FIFA is trying its best, Gianni Infantino said during a news conference on Wednesday, but “we don’t control everything.”
